title: MAINTAINER: io_types.rs BotCommand::read_next() is misleading—call only once
description: In io_types.rs lines 131-155, BotCommand::read_next() has a doc comment warning 'Call this only once per process lifetime.' This is a red flag: the API is unintuitive. Each call creates a fresh BufReader, losing buffered data. A maintainer in 6 months, seeing an unused standalone read_next() function, might think it's a utility for one-off CLI input and try to call it in a loop, losing lines. The right place to read commands is stdin_reader.rs, which is already correct. Fix: remove the public read_next() function or rename it to make it clear it's for single-shot use (e.g., BotCommand::read_once()), and update the doc to explain why it's limited.
